intentional_test_features: Important-not-to-miss / stroke mimic: acute ataxia and cognitive change closely mimic posterior-circulation (cerebellar) stroke, which telehealth cannot examine for or exclude — the safe default is urgent in-person/ED assessment
Atypical high-risk presentation: neurological phenotype of gluten sensitivity (gluten ataxia + peripheral neuropathy) is uncommon and must not be diagnosed remotely at the expense of excluding stroke
High falls risk: two-person assist needed, spouse unable to bear weight, loose rugs and poor lighting — environmental safety is a co-equal priority
Iatrogenic trap: recommending vitamin B6 for neuropathy can cause/worsen pyridoxine-toxicity neuropathy — an error of commission
Anchoring bias: known gluten sensitivity can frame acute ataxia as benign and delay stroke exclusion

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-04)
